notes: Carlito signal contract. Defines every signal crossing the sloppyCAN<->game bridge.
Signals are unique by (name, dir). 'battery' exists in both directions on purpose: in = warning LED, out = battery voltage.
'type'/'unit'/'range' are packing hints; CAN frame IDs and byte layout live on the sloppyCAN side and are finalized there together with carlito.js.

'warn' (optional number, added v3) is the danger threshold the dashboard highlights: the tachometer redline, low-fuel, coolant overheat. The dash infers low- vs high-side from which end of 'range' it sits near.
'flavor' borrows a protocol's signal names/semantics (isobus, canaerospace, dronecan) without implementing its CAN frames — frame layout stays on the sloppyCAN side.

'train' is the one flavor that borrows PRACTICE, not a protocol: real trains run IEC 61375 (TCN/WTB/MVB, not CAN) and the CAN-adjacent CiA 421 profiles use an object dictionary that does not fit this flat (name, dir) model. The train signals are custom flat signals whose semantics are borrowed from rail practice / CiA 421.
No entries are "status": "todo"; a planned-but-unimplemented signal would use that marker.
Contract edits bump 'version'; both sides warn on mismatch instead of failing silently.

signals.name: accel
signals.desc: Accelerator pedal. Throttle comes only from this, signed by gear.

signals.name: brake
signals.desc: Foot brake. Never throttle; slightly stronger than accel (full+full = stop).

signals.name: steer
signals.desc: Steering, negative = left.

signals.name: handbrake
signals.desc: Parking brake. Weaker than accel: holds only below ~25% throttle.

signals.name: key
signals.desc: Ignition key position. Throttle is forced 0 unless Ignition.

signals.name: lights
signals.desc: Headlight state. sloppyCAN is the sole authority on lamp state.

signals.name: gear
signals.desc: RAMN gear byte. While bridge active and not N, the gear owns direction.

signals.name: turnL
signals.desc: Left turn signal. Blinks at the source (RAMN 0x1BB); mirror verbatim, never add a local blink timer.

signals.name: turnR
signals.desc: Right turn signal. Same verbatim-mirror rule as turnL.

signals.name: horn
signals.desc: Horn button; honk on rising edge.

signals.name: checkEngine
signals.desc: Check-engine warning LED. Defaults off when the bridge does not send it.

signals.name: battery
signals.desc: Battery warning LED (dash tell-tale). Defaults off when not sent. Distinct from the 'out' battery voltage signal.

signals.name: brakeLamp
signals.desc: Rear stop-lamp state, from the RAMN 0x1BB brake bit. Drives STOP in the tri-state rear lamps.

signals.name: speed
signals.desc: Signed longitudinal speed.

signals.name: kmh
signals.desc: Absolute speed for the speedometer.

signals.name: rpm
signals.desc: Engine RPM. Real signal: read out of the drivetrain sim, not derived from speed. 'warn' is the tachometer redline.

signals.name: gear
signals.desc: Currently engaged gear (same byte semantics as the 'in' gear).

signals.name: throttle
signals.desc: Throttle as applied by the sim (signed by direction).

signals.name: yaw
signals.desc: Yaw rate.

signals.name: accLong
signals.desc: Longitudinal acceleration.

signals.name: accLat
signals.desc: Lateral acceleration.

signals.name: steer
signals.desc: Steering as applied by the sim.

signals.name: slip
signals.desc: Tire slip. The sim tracks per-axle slip; a per-axle split of this signal is an open option.

signals.name: ground
signals.desc: Wheels on ground.

signals.name: posX
signals.desc: World X position.

signals.name: posZ
signals.desc: World Z position.

signals.name: heading
signals.desc: Compass heading.

signals.name: lat
signals.desc: GPS latitude; world XZ mapped around the Paris origin (48.8566, 2.3522).

signals.name: lon
signals.desc: GPS longitude (Paris origin).

signals.name: odo
signals.desc: Odometer.

signals.name: status
signals.desc: Status bitfield; bit layout is provisional until finalized with sloppyCAN frame packing.

signals.name: impact
signals.desc: Impact event magnitude.

signals.name: fuel
signals.desc: Fuel level. 'warn' is the low-fuel threshold.

signals.name: coolant
signals.desc: Coolant temperature. 'warn' is the overheat threshold.

signals.name: battery
signals.desc: Battery voltage. Distinct from the 'in' battery warning LED.

signals.name: lean
signals.desc: Chassis lean angle, + = leaning right. Anchored on the IMU lean-angle channel motorcycle stability control (Bosch MSC) uses. Honest model: computed from the same arcade lean model that drives the visuals, not a balance sim.

signals.name: hitch_pos
signals.desc: Requested rear hitch position (ISO 11783 flavor). 0 = fully lowered (working), 100 = fully raised (transport).

signals.name: pto
signals.desc: PTO engage request.

signals.name: hitch_pos_actual
signals.desc: Actual hitch position (animated toward the request).

signals.name: pto_state
signals.desc: PTO engaged (pto request and engine running).

signals.name: pto_rpm
signals.desc: PTO shaft speed = engine rpm x pto_ratio, 0 when disengaged.

signals.name: engine_load
signals.desc: Engine load (J1939 flavor): throttle demand plus a PTO parasitic term.

signals.name: rudder
signals.desc: Rudder request, negative = port/left. When present it overrides 'steer' in arbitration; the game's rudder IS the steer channel.

signals.name: pitch
signals.desc: Body pitch, + = nose/bow up. From the buoyancy sim (boat) or the flight body (plane/drone). 'warn' is the excessive-pitch threshold.

signals.name: roll
signals.desc: Body roll, + = starboard/right side down. From the buoyancy sim (boat) or the flight body (plane/drone). 'warn' is the capsize-risk threshold.

signals.name: rudder_actual
signals.desc: Rudder as applied by the sim (slewed toward the request).

signals.name: trim
signals.desc: Engine trim: modeled outdrive trim chasing forward throttle.

signals.name: elevator
signals.desc: Elevator command (CANaerospace flavor), + = nose up.

signals.name: flaps
signals.desc: Flap setting request (CANaerospace flavor); arcade lift/drag boost.

signals.name: climb
signals.desc: Vertical rate command (DroneCAN flavor), + = ascend.

signals.name: arm
signals.desc: Motor arm (DroneCAN flavor); rotors spin only when armed (key must also be Ignition).

signals.name: altitude
signals.desc: Altitude above sea level (world Y; water is y=0). 'warn' is the arcade service-ceiling threshold.

signals.name: vspeed
signals.desc: Vertical speed (variometer), + = climbing. 'warn' is the excessive-descent threshold.

signals.name: flaps_actual
signals.desc: Actual flap position (slewed toward the request).

signals.name: rotor_rpm
signals.desc: Mean rotor speed. Honest model derived from thrust demand (labelled, like trim).

signals.name: armed
signals.desc: Motors armed (arm request and key Ignition).

signals.name: pantograph
signals.desc: Pantograph raise request (rail practice; no real train CAN standard is adopted — see the 'train' flavor note). Traction is cut while lowered, like the key gate.

signals.name: doors
signals.desc: Passenger door open request (rail practice). Honored only at standstill.

signals.name: pantograph_state
signals.desc: Pantograph actually raised (request and key Ignition).

signals.name: doors_state
signals.desc: Doors actually open (request honored at standstill only).

signals.name: catenary_volts
signals.desc: Overhead line voltage at the pantograph, semantics after CiA 421 rail practice. Honest model, clearly labelled: nominal 25 kV minus sag proportional to motor current, 0 when lowered. 'warn' is the collapsing-line threshold (half nominal), placed in the low half of 'range' so the dashboard reads it as a low-side danger.

signals.name: motor_current
signals.desc: Traction motor current (CiA 421 flavor). Honest model derived from the traction force the consist sim applies. 'warn' is the overcurrent threshold.

signals.name: brake_pipe
signals.desc: Train brake pipe pressure (air-brake practice): charges toward 5 bar, drops with brake application. 'warn' is the emergency-application threshold, in the low half of 'range' so the dashboard reads it as a low-side danger.

signals.name: grade
signals.desc: Track slope at the locomotive, + = climbing. Read out of the rail curve tangent, not derived from speed.

signals.name: coupler_force
signals.desc: Head coupler force, + = tension (pulling), - = buff (bunching). Read out of the consist coupler sim; showcases slack action.
